title: Forearm Extensor Muscles
body_html:
MuscleInnervation
Brachioradialis
  • Radial Nerve
Extensor Carpi Radialis Longus
  • Radial Nerve
Extensor Carpi Radialis Brevis
  • Radial Nerve (deep branch)
Extensor digitorum
  • Radial Nerve (posterior interosseous nerve)
Extensor Carpi Ulnaris
  • Radial Nerve (posterior interosseous nerve)
Supinator
  • Radial Nerve (deep branch)
Abductor Pollicis Longus
  • Radial Nerve (posterior interosseous nerve)
Extensor Pollicis Longus
  • Radial Nerve (posterior interosseous nerve)
Extensor Pollicis Brevis
  • Radial Nerve (posterior interosseous nerve)
Extensor Indicis
  • Radial Nerve (posterior interosseous nerve)

The radial nerve divides into deep and superficial branches in the proximal forearm near the radial head. The deep branch becomes the posterior interosseous nerve after passing through the supinator. The superficial branch continues to the hand and doesnt give motor branches in the forearm.

Prior to dividing the radial nerve innervates Brachioradialis and ECRL directly. All other muscles are innervated by the deep branch or its continuation the posterior interosseous nerve.

Image sourced from Wikipedia

Prior to dividing the radial nerve innervates Brachioradialis and ECRL directly. All other muscles are innervated by the deep branch or its continuation the posterior interosseous nerve.
